ending: Ashlock survives the assault on Red Vine Peak, injured but advanced to ninth-stage Star Core. Diana destroys Knox's human body, and Ashlock remakes her surviving infant soul as a black-leaved, nascent-soul-level guardian tree overlooking Ashfallen City. He also discovers that fluid from his black vines can consume the cursed sap. Knox's remains provide the resources to begin a chaos nebula, the first step in Ashlock's alternative inner-world ascent. Completing it requires 10,000 sacrificial credits and Star Cores of five elemental affinities. Elaine refuses Dante's appeal to family loyalty, and Stella cuts his throat despite his warning that his death will bring war within a month. Maple feeds Dante into Ashlock as his body begins a supernova. Ashfallen City is functioning under Redclaw protection, but the Voidmind-Skyrend conflict is urgent. Nox and Stella's Pavilion bounty remain unresolved, and Douglas, Willow, Chaos, and the new Geb are still fighting to rescue the Mudcloaks' home realm.
in_short: Ashlock turns fallen enemies into Ents, expands Ashfallen's alchemy and trading operations, and survives a merchant betrayal that leaves Nox at large and Stella under an Eternal Pursuit Pavilion bounty. A Mystic Realm expedition gives Ashlock the mobile Skyborne Bastions, transforms Elaine and Diana's cultivation, and strengthens Kaida. Ashfallen rescues roughly 100,000 people from a storm over Slymere and builds Ashfallen City for them, while Douglas and the Mudcloaks begin reclaiming their home realm. Knox's hidden ascension causes the storm, and she later frees Dante Voidmind and attacks Red Vine Peak. Diana destroys Knox's body, but Ashlock preserves her soul as a guardian tree. Stella kills Dante after Elaine rejects him. Ashlock reaches ninth-stage Star Core and begins forming an inner world, while Dante's death leaves a Voidmind-Skyrend war imminent.

recaps.text: Previously: Ashlock, a demonic tree rooted at Red Vine Peak, gathered a household and sect around himself while concealing his true nature behind the authority of Ashfallen. Stella Crestfallen became his daughter and public representative, supported by Diana, Elaine, Douglas, Larry, Kaida, and the Redclaw family. Their growing power drew them into Darklight City's conflicts and an alchemy tournament involving the Skyrend and Voidmind families. The confrontation left several nobles dead, placed Ashfallen in possession of valuable bodies and resources, and increased the danger of war between those families. Ashlock entered this volume ready to convert some of the fallen into Ent defenders, expand his influence beyond the peak, and turn the Redclaws' loyalty and Stella's alchemy into a durable organization.

Ashlock consumes Kassandra's corpse and reaches fourth-stage Star Core, then turns Theron, Lillian, and Roderick into the specialized Ents Zeus, Chaos, and Geb. Douglas receives Geb as an earth-working assistant and completes a large alchemy cavern beneath White Stone Palace. Ashfallen formally emerges above the Redclaw family, which will recruit, protect, and manage the new production base. Elder Margret takes charge of the cavern, while Kane Azurecrest and other alchemists accept secrecy obligations. Stella and Ashlock prepare the Ashfallen Trading Company for a merchant meeting. They select useful but politically safer pills and an exclusive beauty treatment, withholding abilities that could expose or threaten the sect. At the same time, Stella develops a coercive pill that hides Ashlock's cursed sap beneath other effects. The intended merchants would receive temporary relief while remaining dependent on Ashfallen. Larry and Kaida leave to hunt, and Elaine finds an unexpectedly large audience waiting for her academy lecture.

The Redclaws reaffirm their loyalty after recognizing that Ashlock is powerful but not all-knowing. Stella completes the layered curse pill, and the company presents itself to Hammond, Lucius, and Nox under Stella's Roselyn identity. The merchants verify the offered pills, but the meeting becomes a struggle over the stock and the curse. Hammond steals Stella's earrings through a portal and is killed by Chaos. Lucius unleashes overwhelming light that devastates White Stone Palace and badly injures Ashfallen's defenders. Nox, who recognized Stella, tries to finish her while she is unconscious, then sacrifices a powerful soul-shadow to escape pursuit. Ashlock possesses a nearby offspring to intervene, uses his Ents and vines to shut away Lucius's sunlight, and executes him. Consuming Hammond advances Ashlock to fifth-stage Star Core. Stella survives with severe burns, while Nox remains loose in Ashlock's forest with stolen property and knowledge of the sect's forces.

Ashlock remakes Lucius as a many-armed light Ent capable of healing, defense, and ranged attacks. The new Ent restores Diana and Elder Margret, but Geb is confirmed destroyed. Nox defeats the pursuing Redclaw Grand Elder, disables Titus, breaches the sect wall, and escapes into the wilderness with one of Stella's earrings. Stella attempts a final pursuit but cannot overcome Nox's shadow armor, forcing Ashlock to extract her. Elsewhere, Dante Voidmind is blamed for the deaths of Skyrend scions and given three months to disprove the accusation or be surrendered to prevent war. Ashlock gains Dimensional Overlap, but Maple redirects its first test into the void. There Ashlock meets Maple's elder siblings, Neptune and Mars. They are world walkers confined by heaven and hope a pact with Ashlock can free them, but his divinity is too weak for the bond. Ashlock learns he holds one divine fragment and that obtaining more will bring both power and danger.

Ashfallen repairs White Stone Palace and prepares for the Mystic Realm. Stella studies spatial techniques recovered from Hammond and trains Elaine for survival, while Ashlock develops Key Resonance as a path toward Spatial Lock. Diana's unstable balance of water and demonic key remains dangerous. A growing movement of frenzied monsters in the northeast is deferred until the expedition returns. Douglas completes a large pavilion settlement and is rewarded as a central builder of the sect. Larry stays behind as a defender, while Stella, Diana, Elaine, Douglas, Kaida, and the Redclaw elders enter the realm with an Ashlock progeny carrying part of his soul. The party immediately divides. Mars takes Elaine for instruction connected to Void Key, and Neptune escorts Diana toward answers about her ancestry. Stella and Maple enter a spatial pocket realm, while the remaining cultivators choose separate environments suited to their own advancement.

Ashlock follows Stella into a sealed Azure Clan training realm and raids its monsters for resources. A system reward raises his resident progeny to Star Core, and he names the new tree Willow Ashfallen. Willow becomes operator of the Skyborne Bastion, a mobile fortress with spatial shields and artillery. The group reaches the Eternal Library, but a Monarch Realm Azure Clan elder catches the intruders. Maple severs the elder's arm, and Ashlock shifts the entire Bastion into a fire realm before the clan can close in. The escape leaves the Azure Clan aware of the intrusion and of Maple's existence. Mars reveals that Elaine is a Voidling whose family cannot form normal nascent souls through Void Key alone. Elaine chooses Illusion as a second affinity and has her cultivation rebuilt from the beginning. Ashlock returns to Red Vine Peak at seventh-stage Star Core after the expedition's gains. He also retrieves Kaida from an ink realm, where the serpent has consumed divine flesh and is ready to evolve.

Kaida evolves into a Midnight Ink Lindworm whose divine ink can reproduce techniques through written intent and heavenly insight. Diana survives a demonic realm trial, replaces her conflicting water and demonic cultivation with unified demonic mist, and reaches Star Core in a stable winged form. Elder Mo and Elder Margret also reach Star Core. Douglas returns as king of the Mudcloaks, a small earth-affinity people with organized construction and siege skills. Using Dimensional Overlap and portals, Ashlock evacuates roughly a thousand Mudcloaks to Red Vine Peak and settles them in the mountain citadel under Douglas. Elaine returns as a Void and Illusion dual cultivator who can insert suggestions through direct contact. Ashlock orders a second, ink-affinity bastion built as a library and refuge for Kaida. He also questions Kane Azurecrest about a possible link to the Azure Clan. Kane's family history is uncertain, and he asks Stella to teach him alchemy.

The shadow merchant's curse takes root in her soul even after purification, and she transfers it into a developing soul-shadow at the cost of blocking safe advancement. Stella tracks the missing earring and leads Willow's Bastion after Nox. The pursuit becomes a prolonged daylight battle in which Ashfallen burns away cover but cannot secure its target. Nox places a large Eternal Pursuit Pavilion bounty on Stella before sundown. Stella severs Nox's hand and recovers the rings containing her earring, but two masked Pavilion hunters answer the bounty. Ashlock draws them onto Willow's fortress, kills the spatial hunter with void tendrils, and traps the shadow hunter inside a hostile light realm. Stella executes the survivor after his heart-demon collapse. Nox escapes before nightfall, and the bounty remains active. Back at Red Vine Peak, the Redclaw Grand Elder reveals his former Pavilion membership. Ashlock consumes one fallen hunter, reaches eighth-stage Star Core, and gains Nocturnal Genesis, which cultivates and heals his root-linked network during deep nightly sleep.

Ashfallen distributes the bounty hunters' artifacts and opens negotiations with Sebastian Silverspire. Sebastian accepts the claim that an unseen immortal speaks through Ashlock and agrees to use the Silverspire family's Pavilion connection. Diana registers under her full name, Diana Ravenborne, as an Iron Seeker, creating a route for Ashfallen to trade pills through the Pavilion. Meanwhile, Ashlock turns the ink tree into a second Skyborne Bastion. The newly conscious operator is named Quill Ashfallen, and the fortress becomes a concealed library and shelter above Red Vine Peak. Quill inherits Ashlock's cultivation method and runic knowledge, though not his active techniques. Kaida discovers a way to write a technique into Quill's bark with divine ink, allowing Quill to absorb heavenly insight. When Ashlock's soul fragment returns from Quill, Ashlock receives the same understanding and finally masters Spatial Lock. Elaine begins acquiring more manuals so Quill can develop into a living defensive array for the peak.

Knox seeks help from the Voidmind family for the curse lodged in her forming soul. Dante's Void Key damages its roots but cannot destroy the central seed, so Knox attempts a hidden nascent soul ascension inside a void chamber. The resulting lightning storm devastates Slymere, while the Voidmind leadership wrongly assumes that Skyrend has attacked and protects only its own residence. Ashlock decides he cannot abandon the city's mortals. He rescues Julian, Catherine, and their daughter, and Stella gives the family a truffle that opens their spirit roots. Stella, Sol, Zeus, Titus, and Ashlock's portal network then evacuate roughly 100,000 people to White Stone Palace. Zeus uses the storm's lightning to hold off the Voidmind elders while Stella conceals her identity. The Redclaw family receives the refugees, and Stella retrieves Zeus after the Voidmind arrays are depleted. Slymere is left empty and damaged, and its leaders remain unaware that Knox's ascension caused the disaster.

Ashlock chooses to keep the Slymere refugees together and orders Douglas and the Mudcloaks to build Ashfallen City north of Red Vine Peak. They finish about 20,000 winter homes, while Ashlock supplies water, waste handling, warmth, light, and food through his plants and roots. Julian accepts a contract to direct the Ashfallen Trading Company's civilian logistics, and Elder Brent stays behind to oversee security. The refugees move into their new settlement under Redclaw administration. When the Mystic Realm opens, Douglas asks Ashlock to help rescue the wider population of Mudcloaks from kobolds. Willow's Bastion enters their home realm and is nearly destroyed by a Titan Tortoise. Chaos kills the creature from inside its eye. Ashlock remakes the corpse as a giant earth-affinity ant and reuses the name Geb for it. Douglas then leads armed Mudcloaks through portals into a breached kobold city, beginning the campaign to reclaim their home.

Knox frees Dante from the Voidmind residence after completing an unstable ascent that leaves the curse consuming her soul. They trace Stella to Red Vine Peak and overpower Ashlock's defenders. Stella returns to protect Ashlock, while Quill prevents Knox from escaping through shadow movement. Knox's twin cores give her overwhelming strength, but every attack accelerates her conversion into wood. Diana arrives in her winged form and destroys Knox's body. Ashlock captures the surviving infant soul and remakes Knox as a black-leaved guardian tree overlooking Ashfallen City. He then learns that digestive fluid from his black vines can consume the cursed sap. Absorbing Knox's remains advances Ashlock to ninth-stage Star Core and begins a chaos nebula that can become an inner world after he gathers five elemental Star Cores and 10,000 sacrificial credits. Elaine rejects Dante's appeal to their shared family. Stella cuts Dante's throat, and Maple feeds him into Ashlock as his body begins a supernova. Dante's death leaves war between Voidmind and Skyrend expected within a month.
